reasoning: The CDC generally reserves Level 3 Travel Health Notices (Reconsider Nonessential Travel) for severe, non-vaccine-preventable diseases or critical health infrastructure collapse (such as Ebola or severe COVID-19 periods). Because yellow fever is easily preventable with a highly effective vaccine, historical precedence shows that even massive yellow fever outbreaks (like Brazil in 2017-2018 or Angola in 2016) typically max out at a Level 2 notice (Practice Enhanced Precautions), primarily advising travelers to get vaccinated. Furthermore, the forecast window is very short (less than six weeks from the April 23, 2025 knowledge cutoff to June 1, 2025). The short timeframe and the fact that an outbreak of a vaccine-preventable disease does not meet the typical CDC criteria for a Level 3 notice make this outcome highly improbable.
